raw_results.title: How the Open Knowledge Format can improve data sharing | Google Cloud Blog
raw_results.content: Official Google Cloud announcement introducing Open Knowledge Format (OKF) as an open, vendor-neutral specification for representing knowledge for AI systems. Says OKF formalizes the LLM-wiki pattern into a portable, interoperable format; v0.1 uses a directory of markdown files with YAML frontmatter. Strong evidence that OKF here refers to Open Knowledge Format.

raw_results.title: Optimizing LLM Accuracy - OpenAI API
raw_results.content: OpenAI documentation explicitly describes retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) as a valuable optimization method for improving LLM accuracy and consistency, calling it additive with prompt engineering rather than mutually exclusive. This is strong evidence that RAG remains relevant in current practice.

raw_results.title: Retrieval-augmented generation for natural language processing: a survey | Artificial Intelligence Review | Springer Nature Link
raw_results.content: Recent survey (published June 1, 2026) notes that LLMs still suffer from hallucination, knowledge-update, and domain-expertise limitations, and states that RAG mitigates these issues by augmenting models with an external knowledge base. Supports the view that RAG remains an active and useful research area.

raw_results.title: Inside OpenAI’s in-house data agent | OpenAI
raw_results.content: OpenAI says its internal data agent pulls relevant embedded context via retrieval-augmented generation instead of scanning raw metadata or logs. This is practical evidence that RAG is still used in production systems, not obsolete.

proceeded_tool_calls_with_reasoning.reasoning: Using general web search to find definitions of OKF in AI or information retrieval context and the relevance status of RAG due to broad availability of recent web information.
proceeded_tool_calls_with_reasoning.result_summary: OKF in this AI context most likely means Open Knowledge Format, a Google Cloud open specification released in June 2026. RAG is not obsolete; current official and recent research sources still describe it as useful and actively supported, though it is evolving alongside longer-context and agentic approaches.

raw_results.title: How the Open Knowledge Format can improve data sharing | Google Cloud Blog
raw_results.content: Official Google Cloud announcement introducing Open Knowledge Format (OKF) as an open, vendor-neutral specification for representing knowledge for AI systems. Says OKF formalizes the LLM-wiki pattern into a portable, interoperable format; v0.1 uses a directory of markdown files with YAML frontmatter. Strong evidence that OKF here refers to Open Knowledge Format.

raw_results.title: Optimizing LLM Accuracy - OpenAI API
raw_results.content: OpenAI documentation explicitly describes retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) as a valuable optimization method for improving LLM accuracy and consistency, calling it additive with prompt engineering rather than mutually exclusive. This is strong evidence that RAG remains relevant in current practice.

raw_results.title: Retrieval-augmented generation for natural language processing: a survey | Artificial Intelligence Review | Springer Nature Link
raw_results.content: Recent survey (published June 1, 2026) notes that LLMs still suffer from hallucination, knowledge-update, and domain-expertise limitations, and states that RAG mitigates these issues by augmenting models with an external knowledge base. Supports the view that RAG remains an active and useful research area.

raw_results.title: Inside OpenAI’s in-house data agent | OpenAI
raw_results.content: OpenAI says its internal data agent pulls relevant embedded context via retrieval-augmented generation instead of scanning raw metadata or logs. This is practical evidence that RAG is still used in production systems, not obsolete.

RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) continues to be highly relevant; it's not obsolete and is actively applied in both research and deployed AI systems.

findings.claim: OKF refers to Open Knowledge Format, designed as an interoperable, open way to encode knowledge for AI, as formalized by Google Cloud.
RAG remains an important method for improving LLM accuracy and consistency, working additively with prompt engineering.
Recent academic surveys confirm that RAG is still considered an active, valuable area for research and practice due to its ability to mitigate LLM limitations.
RAG methods are still used in production at major AI companies, such as OpenAI's in-house data agent.

output_update.final_response:
## Key Points
- OKF stands for Open Knowledge Format, a vendor-neutral, open specification for structuring knowledge for AI systems.
- RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) continues to be highly relevant; it's not obsolete and is actively applied in both research and deployed AI systems.
- RAG helps address ongoing limitations in LLMs, such as hallucination and lack of up-to-date or domain-specific information, by enriching models with external knowledge sources.

## Important Findings
- OKF refers to Open Knowledge Format, designed as an interoperable, open way to encode knowledge for AI, as formalized by Google Cloud. (S1)
- RAG remains an important method for improving LLM accuracy and consistency, working additively with prompt engineering. (S2)
- Recent academic surveys confirm that RAG is still considered an active, valuable area for research and practice due to its ability to mitigate LLM limitations. (S3)
- RAG methods are still used in production at major AI companies, such as OpenAI's in-house data agent. (S4)

## References
- S1: How the Open Knowledge Format can improve data sharing | Google Cloud Blog [web.run] - https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/data-analytics/how-the-open-knowledge-format-can-improve-data-sharing?e=48754805&hl=en
- S2: Optimizing LLM Accuracy - OpenAI API [web.run] - https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/optimizing-llm-accuracy/understanding-the-tools
- S3: Retrieval-augmented generation for natural language processing: a survey | Artificial Intelligence Review | Springer Nature Link [web.run] - https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10462-026-11605-7
- S4: Inside OpenAI’s in-house data agent | OpenAI [web.run] - https://openai.com/index/inside-our-in-house-data-agent/

## Actionable Insights
- For organizations seeking interoperable, portable knowledge representations for AI, consider evaluating or adopting the Open Knowledge Format (OKF).
- AI practitioners and researchers should continue to leverage RAG approaches to address LLM knowledge gaps and improve answer accuracy.

state.final_response:
## Key Points
- OKF stands for Open Knowledge Format, a vendor-neutral, open specification for structuring knowledge for AI systems.
- RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) continues to be highly relevant; it's not obsolete and is actively applied in both research and deployed AI systems.
- RAG helps address ongoing limitations in LLMs, such as hallucination and lack of up-to-date or domain-specific information, by enriching models with external knowledge sources.

## Important Findings
- OKF refers to Open Knowledge Format, designed as an interoperable, open way to encode knowledge for AI, as formalized by Google Cloud. (S1)
- RAG remains an important method for improving LLM accuracy and consistency, working additively with prompt engineering. (S2)
- Recent academic surveys confirm that RAG is still considered an active, valuable area for research and practice due to its ability to mitigate LLM limitations. (S3)
- RAG methods are still used in production at major AI companies, such as OpenAI's in-house data agent. (S4)

## References
- S1: How the Open Knowledge Format can improve data sharing | Google Cloud Blog [web.run] - https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/data-analytics/how-the-open-knowledge-format-can-improve-data-sharing?e=48754805&hl=en
- S2: Optimizing LLM Accuracy - OpenAI API [web.run] - https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/optimizing-llm-accuracy/understanding-the-tools
- S3: Retrieval-augmented generation for natural language processing: a survey | Artificial Intelligence Review | Springer Nature Link [web.run] - https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10462-026-11605-7
- S4: Inside OpenAI’s in-house data agent | OpenAI [web.run] - https://openai.com/index/inside-our-in-house-data-agent/

## Actionable Insights
- For organizations seeking interoperable, portable knowledge representations for AI, consider evaluating or adopting the Open Knowledge Format (OKF).
- AI practitioners and researchers should continue to leverage RAG approaches to address LLM knowledge gaps and improve answer accuracy.

final_state.final_response:
## Key Points
- OKF stands for Open Knowledge Format, a vendor-neutral, open specification for structuring knowledge for AI systems.
- RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) continues to be highly relevant; it's not obsolete and is actively applied in both research and deployed AI systems.
- RAG helps address ongoing limitations in LLMs, such as hallucination and lack of up-to-date or domain-specific information, by enriching models with external knowledge sources.

## Important Findings
- OKF refers to Open Knowledge Format, designed as an interoperable, open way to encode knowledge for AI, as formalized by Google Cloud. (S1)
- RAG remains an important method for improving LLM accuracy and consistency, working additively with prompt engineering. (S2)
- Recent academic surveys confirm that RAG is still considered an active, valuable area for research and practice due to its ability to mitigate LLM limitations. (S3)
- RAG methods are still used in production at major AI companies, such as OpenAI's in-house data agent. (S4)

## References
- S1: How the Open Knowledge Format can improve data sharing | Google Cloud Blog [web.run] - https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/data-analytics/how-the-open-knowledge-format-can-improve-data-sharing?e=48754805&hl=en
- S2: Optimizing LLM Accuracy - OpenAI API [web.run] - https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/optimizing-llm-accuracy/understanding-the-tools
- S3: Retrieval-augmented generation for natural language processing: a survey | Artificial Intelligence Review | Springer Nature Link [web.run] - https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10462-026-11605-7
- S4: Inside OpenAI’s in-house data agent | OpenAI [web.run] - https://openai.com/index/inside-our-in-house-data-agent/

## Actionable Insights
- For organizations seeking interoperable, portable knowledge representations for AI, consider evaluating or adopting the Open Knowledge Format (OKF).
- AI practitioners and researchers should continue to leverage RAG approaches to address LLM knowledge gaps and improve answer accuracy.
